```
+# shellcheck disable=SC2148,SC2034,SC2125,SC1017
+# GlossaDocs frontend (root) – copy to .env for local non-Docker runs
+# Used by: npm run dev (Vite)
+
+# Backend API base URL (browser will call this)
+VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:4000
+
+# Keycloak OIDC token endpoint (for dev password flow)
+VITE_OIDC_TOKEN_URL=http://localhost:8080/realms/glossadocs/protocol/openid-connect/token
+
+# Keycloak client id
+VITE_OIDC_CLIENT_ID=glossadocs-api

index af3ba2e..24402e2 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -169,6 +169,19 @@ For current local/dev usage this is acceptable. For longer-running environments,
 - Partitioning by time with periodic partition drop.
 - Archival pipeline to move old audit rows to cheaper storage.
 
+### Deploying to AWS Lambda
+
+The backend can run on AWS Lambda behind API Gateway. Use the Lambda entrypoint:
+
+- **Handler**: &#96;dist/lambda.handler&#96; (or &#96;lambda.handler&#96; if the deployment package root is &#96;dist/&#96;).
+- Set Lambda environment variables (or use Secrets Manager for &#96;DATABASE_URL&#96;): &#96;NODE_ENV&#96;, &#96;API_PORT&#96;, &#96;CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS&#96;, &#96;DATABASE_URL&#96;, &#96;OIDC_ISSUER_URL&#96;, &#96;OIDC_AUDIENCE&#96;, &#96;OIDC_JWKS_URL&#96;.
+
+Deploy the built backend (e.g. &#96;dist/&#96;, &#96;migrations/&#96;, &#96;node_modules&#96;) as the Lambda function code and point API Gateway HTTP API (or REST API) at this handler.
+
+### Document encryption at rest
+
+Document title and content can be encrypted in PostgreSQL so that anyone with DB access cannot read them without the key. Set &#96;DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY&#96; (base64-encoded 32-byte key) in the backend environment. See [docs/architecture/document-encryption.md](docs/architecture/document-encryption.md) for design and key management.
+
 ## Current Limitations
 
 - Backend exists, but frontend authentication is still in temporary dev mode (not real OIDC login flow yet).
diff --git a/backend/.env.example b/backend/.env.example
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..586534d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/backend/.env.example
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+# shellcheck disable=SC2148,SC2034,SC2125,SC1017
+# GlossaDocs backend – copy to .env for local non-Docker runs
+# Used by: npm run dev (backend), migrate:up, start
+
+NODE_ENV=development
+API_PORT=4000
+CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=*
+
+# PostgreSQL connection string (required for documents + settings)
+DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/glossadocs
+
+# Keycloak OIDC (required for JWT verification)
+OIDC_ISSUER_URL=http://localhost:8080/realms/glossadocs
+OIDC_AUDIENCE=glossadocs-api
+# Optional: defaults to OIDC_ISSUER_URL/protocol/openid-connect/certs
+# OIDC_JWKS_URL=http://localhost:8080/realms/glossadocs/protocol/openid-connect/certs
+
+# Optional: base64-encoded 32-byte key for encrypting document title/content at rest.
+# Generate with: node -e "console.log(require('crypto').randomBytes(32).toString('base64'))"
+# DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY=

diff --git a/backend/src/lambda.ts b/backend/src/lambda.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..86a33d8
--- /dev/null
+++ b/backend/src/lambda.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+/**
+ * AWS Lambda entrypoint for GlossaDocs API.
+ * Use this handler with API Gateway HTTP API or REST API (Lambda proxy integration).
+ * Config is read from Lambda environment variables (same as getConfig()).
+ */
+import awsLambdaFastify from "@fastify/aws-lambda";
+import { buildApp } from "./app.js";
+import { getConfig } from "./shared/config.js";
+
+const config = getConfig();
+const app = buildApp(config);
+const proxy = awsLambdaFastify(app);
+
+await app.ready();
+
+export const handler = proxy;
diff --git a/backend/src/modules/documents/document-service.ts b/backend/src/modules/documents/document-service.ts
index 83a320e..34701c9 100644

+++ b/backend/src/modules/documents/document-service.ts
@@ -1,4 +1,8 @@
 import { ApiError } from "../../shared/api-error.js";
+import {

diff --git a/backend/src/shared/document-encryption.ts b/backend/src/shared/document-encryption.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c2fd35d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/backend/src/shared/document-encryption.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+/**
+ * AES-256-GCM encryption for document title and content at rest.
+ * Used by PgDocumentRepository when DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY is set.
+ *
+ * This module never logs or persists the key; it only receives it as an argument.
+ * Key theft risk is in how the key is stored and loaded (env, secret store, logging).
+ */
+import { createCipheriv, createDecipheriv, randomBytes } from "node:crypto";
+
+const ALGORITHM = "aes-256-gcm";
+const IV_LENGTH = 12;
+const AUTH_TAG_LENGTH = 16;
+const VERSION_PREFIX = "v1:";
+
+function checkKey(key: Buffer): void {
+  if (key.length !== 32) {
+    throw new Error("Document encryption key must be 32 bytes (256 bits)");
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Encrypt plaintext with the given key. Returns a versioned, base64-encoded string
+ * suitable for storing in a text column: "v1:" + base64(IV || ciphertext || authTag).
+ */
+export function encryptDocumentField(plaintext: string, key: Buffer): string {
+  checkKey(key);
+  const iv = randomBytes(IV_LENGTH);
+  const cipher = createCipheriv(ALGORITHM, key, iv, { authTagLength: AUTH_TAG_LENGTH });
+  const encrypted = Buffer.concat([cipher.update(plaintext, "utf8"), cipher.final()]);
+  const authTag = cipher.getAuthTag();
+  const blob = Buffer.concat([iv, encrypted, authTag]);
+  return VERSION_PREFIX + blob.toString("base64");
+}
+
+/**
+ * Decrypt a value from the database. If the value starts with "v1:", it is
+ * decrypted; otherwise it is returned as-is (legacy plaintext).
+ */
+export function decryptDocumentField(ciphertext: string, key: Buffer): string {
+  if (!ciphertext.startsWith(VERSION_PREFIX)) {
+    return ciphertext;
+  }
+  checkKey(key);
+  const blob = Buffer.from(ciphertext.slice(VERSION_PREFIX.length), "base64");
+  if (blob.length &lt; IV_LENGTH + AUTH_TAG_LENGTH) {
+    throw new Error("Document field ciphertext too short to be valid");
+  }
+  const iv = blob.subarray(0, IV_LENGTH);
+  const authTag = blob.subarray(blob.length - AUTH_TAG_LENGTH);
+  const encrypted = blob.subarray(IV_LENGTH, blob.length - AUTH_TAG_LENGTH);
+  const decipher = createDecipheriv(ALGORITHM, key, iv, { authTagLength: AUTH_TAG_LENGTH });
+  decipher.setAuthTag(authTag);
+  return decipher.update(encrypted) + decipher.final("utf8");
+}
+
+/**
+ * Parse a base64-encoded 32-byte key. Returns null if the string is empty/whitespace.
+ */
+export function parseDocumentEncryptionKey(encoded: string | undefined): Buffer | null {
+  if (encoded === undefined || encoded.trim() === "") {
+    return null;
+  }
+  const key = Buffer.from(encoded.trim(), "base64");
+  if (key.length !== 32) {
+    throw new Error(
+      &#96;DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY must be base64 of 32 bytes (got ${key.length} bytes after decode)&#96;
+    );
+  }
+  return key;
+}
diff --git a/backend/src/shared/document-sanitizer.ts b/backend/src/shared/document-sanitizer.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..3252e15
--- /dev/null
+++ b/backend/src/shared/document-sanitizer.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,43 @@
+/**
+ * Server-side HTML sanitization for document title and content.
+ * Applied on the document write path to prevent stored XSS and dangerous markup.
+ */
+import sanitizeHtml from "sanitize-html";
+
+/** Strip all HTML from title; return plain text. */
+export function sanitizeDocumentTitle(title: string): string {
+  return sanitizeHtml(title, { allowedTags: [], allowedAttributes: {} });
+}
+
+/** Allowed tags for rich-text document content (editor output). */
+const ALLOWED_CONTENT_TAGS = [
+  "p",
+  "div",
+  "br",
+  "span",
+  "strong",
+  "b",
+  "em",
+  "i",
+  "u",
+  "img"
+];
+
+/** Allowed attributes per tag for document content. */
+const ALLOWED_CONTENT_ATTRIBUTES: Record&lt;string, string[]&gt; = {
+  span: ["style"],
+  div: ["style"],
+  p: ["style"],
+  img: ["src", "alt", "width", "height", "style"]
+};
+
+/** Sanitize document HTML content; allow only safe tags and attributes. */
+export function sanitizeDocumentContent(content: string): string {
+  return sanitizeHtml(content, {
+    allowedTags: ALLOWED_CONTENT_TAGS,
+    allowedAttributes: ALLOWED_CONTENT_ATTRIBUTES,
+    allowedSchemes: ["http", "https", "data"],
+    allowedSchemesByTag: { img: ["http", "https", "data"] },
+    allowedSchemesAppliedToAttributes: ["src"]
+  });
+}
diff --git a/backend/src/shared/sanitize-html.d.ts b/backend/src/shared/sanitize-html.d.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..686667a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/backend/src/shared/sanitize-html.d.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+declare module "sanitize-html" {
+  interface Options {
+    allowedTags?: string[];
+    allowedAttributes?: Record&lt;string, string[]&gt;;
+    allowedSchemes?: string[];
+    allowedSchemesByTag?: Record&lt;string, string[]&gt;;
+    allowedSchemesAppliedToAttributes?: string[];
+  }
+  function sanitizeHtml(html: string, options?: Options): string;
+  export = sanitizeHtml;
+}
diff --git a/backend/test/document-encryption.test.ts b/backend/test/document-encryption.test.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1d0de15
--- /dev/null
+++ b/backend/test/document-encryption.test.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,96 @@
+import { randomBytes } from "node:crypto";
+import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
+
+import {
+  decryptDocumentField,
+  encryptDocumentField,
+  parseDocumentEncryptionKey
+} from "../src/shared/document-encryption.js";
+
+function validKey(): Buffer {
+  return randomBytes(32);
+}
+
+describe("document-encryption", () =&gt; {
+  describe("encryptDocumentField / decryptDocumentField", () =&gt; {
+    it("roundtrips plaintext", () =&gt; {
+      const key = validKey();
+      const plain = "Hello, world.";
+      const encrypted = encryptDocumentField(plain, key);
+      expect(encrypted).toMatch(/^v1:/);
+      expect(encrypted).not.toBe(plain);
+      expect(decryptDocumentField(encrypted, key)).toBe(plain);
+    });
+
+    it("produces different ciphertext each time (random IV)", () =&gt; {
+      const key = validKey();
+      const a = encryptDocumentField("same", key);
+      const b = encryptDocumentField("same", key);
+      expect(a).not.toBe(b);
+      expect(decryptDocumentField(a, key)).toBe("same");
+      expect(decryptDocumentField(b, key)).toBe("same");
+    });
+
+    it("decrypts empty string", () =&gt; {
+      const key = validKey();
+      const encrypted = encryptDocumentField("", key);
+      expect(decryptDocumentField(encrypted, key)).toBe("");
+    });
+
+    it("decrypts long content (e.g. document body)", () =&gt; {
+      const key = validKey();
+      const long = "&lt;p&gt;" + "x".repeat(10000) + "&lt;/p&gt;";
+      expect(decryptDocumentField(encryptDocumentField(long, key), key)).toBe(long);
+    });
+
+    it("returns legacy plaintext unchanged when value does not start with v1:", () =&gt; {
+      const key = validKey();
+      expect(decryptDocumentField("plain title", key)).toBe("plain title");
+      expect(decryptDocumentField("", key)).toBe("");
+    });
+
+    it("throws when decrypting with wrong key (confidentiality)", () =&gt; {
+      const key1 = validKey();
+      const key2 = validKey();
+      const encrypted = encryptDocumentField("secret", key1);
+      expect(() =&gt; decryptDocumentField(encrypted, key2)).toThrow();
+    });
+
+    it("throws when ciphertext is tampered (integrity)", () =&gt; {
+      const key = validKey();
+      const encrypted = encryptDocumentField("secret", key);
+      const tampered = encrypted.slice(0, -4) + "xxxx";
+      expect(() =&gt; decryptDocumentField(tampered, key)).toThrow();
+    });
+
+    it("throws when key is not 32 bytes", () =&gt; {
+      const shortKey = Buffer.alloc(16);
+      expect(() =&gt; encryptDocumentField("x", shortKey)).toThrow("32 bytes");
+      const longKey = Buffer.alloc(64);
+      expect(() =&gt; encryptDocumentField("x", longKey)).toThrow("32 bytes");
+    });
+  });
+
+  describe("parseDocumentEncryptionKey", () =&gt; {
+    it("returns null for undefined or empty string", () =&gt; {
+      expect(parseDocumentEncryptionKey(undefined)).toBeNull();
+      expect(parseDocumentEncryptionKey("")).toBeNull();
+      expect(parseDocumentEncryptionKey("   ")).toBeNull();
+    });
+
+    it("returns 32-byte buffer for valid base64 key", () =&gt; {
+      const key = randomBytes(32);
+      const encoded = key.toString("base64");
+      const parsed = parseDocumentEncryptionKey(encoded);
+      expect(parsed).not.toBeNull();
+      expect(Buffer.isBuffer(parsed)).toBe(true);
+      expect(parsed!.length).toBe(32);
+      expect(parsed!.equals(key)).toBe(true);
+    });
+
+    it("throws when base64 decodes to wrong length", () =&gt; {
+      const badKey = Buffer.alloc(16).toString("base64");
+      expect(() =&gt; parseDocumentEncryptionKey(badKey)).toThrow("32 bytes");
+    });
+  });
+});
diff --git a/backend/test/document-sanitizer.test.ts b/backend/test/document-sanitizer.test.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e736e5f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/backend/test/document-sanitizer.test.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,117 @@
+import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
+
+import {
+  sanitizeDocumentContent,
+  sanitizeDocumentTitle
+} from "../src/shared/document-sanitizer.js";
+
+describe("sanitizeDocumentTitle", () =&gt; {
+  it("returns empty string for empty input", () =&gt; {
+    expect(sanitizeDocumentTitle("")).toBe("");
+  });
+
+  it("strips all HTML and returns plain text only", () =&gt; {
+    expect(sanitizeDocumentTitle("Hello")).toBe("Hello");
+    expect(sanitizeDocumentTitle("&lt;b&gt;Hello&lt;/b&gt;")).toBe("Hello");
+    expect(sanitizeDocumentTitle("&lt;p&gt;Para&lt;/p&gt;")).toBe("Para");
+    expect(sanitizeDocumentTitle("A &lt;span&gt;B&lt;/span&gt; C")).toBe("A B C");
+  });
+
+  it("removes script tags and their content so no executable code remains", () =&gt; {
+    const out = sanitizeDocumentTitle("&lt;script&gt;alert(1)&lt;/script&gt;Title");
+    expect(out).not.toContain("script");
+    expect(out).not.toContain("alert");
+    expect(out).toContain("Title");
+  });
+
+  it("removes event handler attributes so title cannot trigger script on render", () =&gt; {
+    const out = sanitizeDocumentTitle('&lt;img onerror="alert(1)" src="x"&gt;');
+    expect(out).not.toContain("onerror");
+    expect(out).not.toContain("alert");
+  });
+
+  it("removes style tags and their content", () =&gt; {
+    const out = sanitizeDocumentTitle("&lt;style&gt;.x{color:red}&lt;/style&gt;Title");
+    expect(out).not.toContain("style");
+    expect(out).not.toContain("color");
+    expect(out).toContain("Title");
+  });
+});
+
+describe("sanitizeDocumentContent", () =&gt; {
+  it("preserves safe editor tags and strips nothing from allowed set", () =&gt; {
+    const safe = "&lt;p&gt;Hello&lt;/p&gt;&lt;div&gt;&lt;strong&gt;bold&lt;/strong&gt; &lt;em&gt;italic&lt;/em&gt; &lt;u&gt;underline&lt;/u&gt;&lt;/div&gt;";
+    expect(sanitizeDocumentContent(safe)).toBe(safe);
+  });
+
+  it("preserves br and span with style", () =&gt; {
+    const withBr = "&lt;p&gt;Line one&lt;br&gt;Line two&lt;/p&gt;";
+    const outBr = sanitizeDocumentContent(withBr);
+    expect(outBr).toContain("Line one");
+    expect(outBr).toContain("Line two");
+    expect(outBr).toMatch(/&lt;br\s*\/?&gt;/);
+    const withSpan = '&lt;span style="font-size: 14px"&gt;Text&lt;/span&gt;';
+    const outSpan = sanitizeDocumentContent(withSpan);
+    expect(outSpan).toContain("&lt;span");
+    expect(outSpan).toContain("style=");
+    expect(outSpan).toContain("font-size");
+    expect(outSpan).toContain("Text");
+    expect(outSpan).toContain("&lt;/span&gt;");
+  });
+
+  it("preserves img with allowed attributes and strips disallowed", () =&gt; {
+    const img = '&lt;img src="https://example.com/a.png" alt="An image" width="100" height="50"&gt;';
+    expect(sanitizeDocumentContent(img)).toContain("src=");
+    expect(sanitizeDocumentContent(img)).toContain("alt=");
+    expect(sanitizeDocumentContent(img)).not.toContain("onerror");
+  });
+
+  it("removes script tags so stored content cannot run script when rendered", () =&gt; {
+    const malicious = "&lt;p&gt;OK&lt;/p&gt;&lt;script&gt;document.steal = 1;&lt;/script&gt;&lt;p&gt;OK&lt;/p&gt;";
+    const out = sanitizeDocumentContent(malicious);
+    expect(out).not.toContain("script");
+    expect(out).not.toContain("steal");
+    expect(out).toContain("OK");
+  });
+
+  it("removes inline event handlers so content cannot trigger script", () =&gt; {
+    const withHandler = '&lt;p onclick="alert(1)"&gt;Click&lt;/p&gt;';
+    const out = sanitizeDocumentContent(withHandler);
+    expect(out).not.toContain("onclick");
+    expect(out).not.toContain("alert");
+    expect(out).toContain("Click");
+  });
+
+  it("removes javascript: URLs from links and img src so no script execution via URL", () =&gt; {
+    const jsLink = '&lt;a href="javascript:alert(1)"&gt;Link&lt;/a&gt;';
+    const out = sanitizeDocumentContent(jsLink);
+    expect(out).not.toContain("javascript:");
+    expect(out).not.toContain("alert");
+    const jsImg = '&lt;img src="javascript:alert(1)"&gt;';
+    const outImg = sanitizeDocumentContent(jsImg);
+    expect(outImg).not.toContain("javascript:");
+  });
+
+  it("removes iframe and object so no embedded content can run", () =&gt; {
+    const iframe = '&lt;iframe src="https://evil.com"&gt;&lt;/iframe&gt;&lt;p&gt;Text&lt;/p&gt;';
+    const out = sanitizeDocumentContent(iframe);
+    expect(out).not.toContain("iframe");
+    expect(out).not.toContain("evil");
+    expect(out).toContain("Text");
+  });
+
+  it("removes style tags so injected CSS cannot run (e.g. expression or behavior)", () =&gt; {
+    const withStyle = "&lt;style&gt;body { background: url('javascript:alert(1)'); }&lt;/style&gt;&lt;p&gt;X&lt;/p&gt;";
+    const out = sanitizeDocumentContent(withStyle);
+    expect(out).not.toContain("style");
+    expect(out).not.toContain("javascript:");
+    expect(out).toContain("X");
+  });
+
+  it("allows data URLs for img src when used for inline images", () =&gt; {
+    const dataImg = '&lt;img src="data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo=" alt="x"&gt;';
+    const out = sanitizeDocumentContent(dataImg);
+    expect(out).toContain("data:");
+    expect(out).toContain("src=");
+  });
+});
diff --git a/backend/test/document-service.test.ts b/backend/test/document-service.test.ts
index 6268cff..f8677cf 100644
--- a/backend/test/document-service.test.ts
+++ b/backend/test/document-service.test.ts
@@ -31,6 +31,44 @@ class EmptyRepository implements DocumentRepository {
   }
 }
 
+/** Records the last payload passed to insert/updateOwned so tests can assert sanitization. */
+class RecordingRepository implements DocumentRepository {
+  public lastInsertPayload: CreateDocumentDto | null = null;
+  public lastUpdatePatch: UpdateDocumentDto | null = null;
+
+  private readonly doc: DocumentAggregate = {
+    id: "00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000001",
+    ownerId: "actor-1",
+    title: "",
+    content: "",
+    language: "en",
+    createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
+    updatedAt: new Date().toISOString()
+  };
+
+  public async findByOwner(_actorSub: string): Promise&lt;DocumentAggregate[]&gt; {
+    return [this.doc];
+  }
+  public async findOwnedById(_actorSub: string, id: string): Promise&lt;DocumentAggregate | null&gt; {
+    return id === this.doc.id ? this.doc : null;
+  }
+  public async insert(actorSub: string, payload: CreateDocumentDto): Promise&lt;DocumentAggregate&gt; {
+    this.lastInsertPayload = { ...payload };
+    return { ...this.doc, ownerId: actorSub, title: payload.title, content: payload.content };
+  }
+  public async updateOwned(
+    _actorSub: string,
+    _id: string,
+    patch: UpdateDocumentDto
+  ): Promise&lt;DocumentAggregate | null&gt; {
+    this.lastUpdatePatch = { ...patch };
+    return { ...this.doc };
+  }
+  public async deleteOwned(_actorSub: string, _id: string): Promise&lt;boolean&gt; {
+    return false;
+  }
+}
+

+describe("DocumentService sanitization", () =&gt; {
+  it("createOwned passes sanitized title and content to repository (no script or HTML in title)", async () =&gt; {
+    const repo = new RecordingRepository();
+    const service = new DocumentService(repo);
+    const payload: CreateDocumentDto = {
+      title: "&lt;script&gt;alert(1)&lt;/script&gt;Real title",
+      content: "&lt;p&gt;Safe&lt;/p&gt;",
+      language: "en"
+    };
+
+    await service.createOwned("actor-1", payload);
+
+    expect(repo.lastInsertPayload).not.toBeNull();
+    expect(repo.lastInsertPayload!.title).not.toContain("script");
+    expect(repo.lastInsertPayload!.title).not.toContain("alert");
+    expect(repo.lastInsertPayload!.title).toContain("Real title");
+    expect(repo.lastInsertPayload!.content).toBe("&lt;p&gt;Safe&lt;/p&gt;");
+  });
+
+  it("createOwned passes sanitized content to repository (script and handlers stripped)", async () =&gt; {
+    const repo = new RecordingRepository();
+    const service = new DocumentService(repo);
+    const payload: CreateDocumentDto = {
+      title: "Doc",
+      content: "&lt;p&gt;OK&lt;/p&gt;&lt;script&gt;evil()&lt;/script&gt;&lt;div onclick=\"bad()\"&gt;X&lt;/div&gt;",
+      language: "en"
+    };
+
+    await service.createOwned("actor-1", payload);
+
+    expect(repo.lastInsertPayload!.content).not.toContain("script");
+    expect(repo.lastInsertPayload!.content).not.toContain("evil");
+    expect(repo.lastInsertPayload!.content).not.toContain("onclick");
+    expect(repo.lastInsertPayload!.content).not.toContain("bad");
+    expect(repo.lastInsertPayload!.content).toContain("OK");
+    expect(repo.lastInsertPayload!.content).toContain("X");
+  });
+
+  it("updateOwned passes sanitized title when title is provided", async () =&gt; {
+    const repo = new RecordingRepository();
+    const service = new DocumentService(repo);
+
+    await service.updateOwned("actor-1", "00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000001", {
+      title: "&lt;b&gt;Bold title&lt;/b&gt;"
+    });
+
+    expect(repo.lastUpdatePatch).not.toBeNull();
+    expect(repo.lastUpdatePatch!.title).not.toContain("&lt;");
+    expect(repo.lastUpdatePatch!.title).not.toContain("&gt;");
+    expect(repo.lastUpdatePatch!.title).toContain("Bold title");
+  });
+
+  it("updateOwned passes sanitized content when content is provided", async () =&gt; {
+    const repo = new RecordingRepository();
+    const service = new DocumentService(repo);
+
+    await service.updateOwned("actor-1", "00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000001", {
+      content: "&lt;p&gt;Fine&lt;/p&gt;&lt;iframe src=\"https://evil.com\"&gt;&lt;/iframe&gt;"
+    });
+
+    expect(repo.lastUpdatePatch!.content).not.toContain("iframe");
+    expect(repo.lastUpdatePatch!.content).toContain("Fine");
+  });
+});

diff --git a/docs/architecture/document-encryption.md b/docs/architecture/document-encryption.md

index 0000000..7571f39
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/architecture/document-encryption.md
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+# Document encryption at rest (PostgreSQL)
+
+## Goal
+
+Protect document title and content so that anyone with access to the database (backup leak, stolen credentials, insider) cannot read plaintext without the encryption key.
+
+## Scope
+
+- **Encrypted**: &#96;documents.title&#96;, &#96;documents.content&#96;
+- **Not encrypted**: &#96;documents.id&#96;, &#96;owner_id&#96;, &#96;language&#96;, &#96;created_at&#96;, &#96;updated_at&#96; (needed for querying, indexing, and ownership; no sensitive content)
+
+## Algorithm and format
+
+- **Algorithm**: AES-256-GCM (authenticated encryption: confidentiality and integrity).
+- **Key**: 32-byte (256-bit) key. Supplied via environment (e.g. &#96;DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY&#96; as base64) or from a secret store (e.g. AWS Secrets Manager). Never committed to code.
+- **Per-field storage format**: Each encrypted value is stored as:
+  - Prefix: &#96;v1:&#96; (version; allows future algorithm or format changes and distinguishes from legacy plaintext).
+  - Body: base64(IV || ciphertext || authTag).
+    - IV: 12 bytes, random per encryption (Node crypto default for GCM).
+    - authTag: 16 bytes, produced by GCM.
+- **Legacy**: If the key is configured but a stored value does not start with &#96;v1:&#96;, it is treated as plaintext (existing rows before encryption was enabled). New and updated rows are always written encrypted when a key is set.
+
+## Where it runs
+
+- **Layer**: Encryption and decryption happen only in the **repository** (&#96;PgDocumentRepository&#96;). The document service and API see only plaintext; the repository encrypts on write and decrypts on read.
+- **Key optional**: If &#96;DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY&#96; is not set, the repository does not encrypt or decrypt (backward compatibility and local dev without a key).
+
+## Key management
+
+- **Development**: Optional. Set &#96;DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY&#96; (base64 of 32 random bytes) in &#96;.env&#96; to test encryption. Generate with e.g. &#96;node -e "console.log(require('crypto').randomBytes(32).toString('base64'))"&#96;.
+- **Production (e.g. AWS Lambda)**: Prefer storing the key in AWS Secrets Manager (or Parameter Store). Fetch at cold start or inject into Lambda env; pass the key (or null) into the repository constructor. Never log or expose the key.
+
+## Schema
+
+No migration required. Existing &#96;documents.title&#96; and &#96;documents.content&#96; columns (type &#96;text&#96;) hold either plaintext (legacy) or the &#96;v1:&#96; + base64-encrypted value. No new columns.
+
+## Migrating existing data
+
+To encrypt existing plaintext rows:
+
+1. Ensure &#96;DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY&#96; is set.
+2. Run a one-off job (script, Lambda, or migration task) that: for each row, if &#96;title&#96; or &#96;content&#96; does not start with &#96;v1:&#96;, encrypt the value and update the row. The application will then decrypt on read regardless.
+
+(Optional; the app already supports mixed legacy plaintext + new encrypted rows.)
+
+## Security notes
+
+- **Key compromise**: If the key is lost, encrypted data cannot be recovered. If the key is stolen, an attacker can decrypt. Protect the key with the same rigor as the database credentials.
+- **Integrity**: GCM authentication detects tampering; corrupted or modified ciphertext will fail to decrypt.
+- **No search**: Encrypted content cannot be searched in the DB (e.g. full-text search). Queries remain on &#96;owner_id&#96;, &#96;id&#96;, &#96;language&#96;, and timestamps.
+
+## Key backup and recovery
+
+**Is “key lost = data gone” standard?** Yes. With symmetric encryption (one key for both encrypt and decrypt), only the key holder can read the data. So by design, if the key is gone, no one can decrypt—including you. That’s the same tradeoff as any system that encrypts data at rest (e.g. RDS encryption, encrypted backups).
+
+**Practical mitigation:** Treat the key like a critical credential and back it up securely, the same way you protect database credentials or API keys:
+
+- **Production**: Store the key in a secret manager (e.g. AWS Secrets Manager). Use a second, separate backup (e.g. another region, or an offline copy in a secure vault) if your policy requires recovery from total loss of the primary store. Restrict access with IAM or equivalent so only the app (and designated operators) can read it.
+- **Document where the key lives** and who can access it, so a future team member or restore process can find it.
+- **Rotation**: If you ever need to change the key, you must re-encrypt all rows with the new key (decrypt with old key, encrypt with new key). Plan that as a one-off migration; the current design uses a single key.
+
+There is no built-in “recovery” that avoids the key: any such back door would weaken security.
+
+## Single key and symmetric encryption
+
+**Is one key + symmetric encryption insecure?** No. AES-256-GCM with a single key is standard and widely used (e.g. TLS, disk encryption, many apps). The algorithm and "one key" design are not the weak point. The risk is **key management**: where the key is stored, who can read it, and how it gets into the process.
+
+**Where could someone steal the key?** The encryption code never stores or logs the key; it only receives it as an argument. The key enters the app from **configuration** (e.g. &#96;DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY&#96; in the environment, or from a secret store). Theft is possible if:
+
+- **Env or config is exposed**: On some systems, process environment is visible (e.g. &#96;ps eww&#96; or monitoring that dumps env). Avoid putting the raw key in env on shared hosts if you can; prefer fetching from a secret manager at startup and keeping it only in memory.
+- **Logging**: Anything that logs &#96;process.env&#96; or the full config object could leak the key. The app must never log secrets.
+- **Where the key is stored**: If the key lives in a &#96;.env&#96; file, anyone with file access can read it. If it's in AWS Secrets Manager (or similar), only identities with access to that secret can fetch it. So "stealing" usually means compromising the store (file, CI/CD, IAM, etc.), not the encryption code itself.
+- **Memory**: While the process runs, the key exists in memory. A memory dump (crash dump, forensic image) could contain it. Mitigate by restricting who can capture memory and by not logging or persisting the key.
+
+So: the code in &#96;document-encryption.ts&#96; does not add new ways to steal the key; it only uses a key it is given. Secure the place the key comes from (secret store, env, access control) and avoid logging or exposing config.
+
+Backup the key; don't rely on a single copy.

```
</details>
</tool-use>

---

### Overall review scope

I reviewed the diff from `main` → `feature/full-backend-integration`, plus the backend/docs described in `README.md` and `docs/architecture`. The main changes are: document encryption at rest, HTML sanitization for document content/title, AWS Lambda support, Docker healthchecks, and minor config/env wiring.

I did **not** see obvious frontend changes in this diff; if there are additional frontend files specific to this branch (e.g. new API client, hooks, or components), please point me at them so I can review that side too.

---

### Potential issues and improvement items

#### 1. Security (backend)

- **Document encryption key handling can hard‑fail startup**  
  - **What**: `parseDocumentEncryptionKey` throws if `DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY` is non-empty but not a base64-encoded 32‑byte value. `buildApp` calls this at startup, so a misconfigured env will crash the whole process.  
  - **Why it matters**: Operationally brittle; a typo in env or a secret rotated to the wrong length will take the service down completely. From a security perspective this is fine, but availability suffers.  
  - **Suggestion**: Consider either:
    - Logging a clear error and refusing to start (but with a more controlled error path / health status), or
    - Falling back to `null` (no encryption) with a loud log + metric in *non‑production*, while still hard‑failing in `NODE_ENV=production`.  
    Clarify expected behavior in docs.

- **Sanitization policy is backend-only; potential mismatch with frontend expectations**  
  - **What**: `DocumentService` now sanitizes title/content on create/update using `sanitize-html` with a fairly strict allowlist.  
  - **Why it matters**: Good for stored XSS prevention, but if the frontend editor allows tags/attributes beyond this set, users might see their formatting unexpectedly stripped after save. This is not insecure but is a **UX correctness** concern.  
  - **Suggestion**:
    - Make sure the frontend editor’s allowed formatting exactly matches `ALLOWED_CONTENT_TAGS` / `ALLOWED_CONTENT_ATTRIBUTES`.  
    - Optionally expose this policy as shared config or document it clearly for frontend devs.

- **`CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=*` in backend `.env.example`**  
  - **What**: Example config uses wildcard CORS.  
  - **Why it matters**: Fine for local/dev but dangerous if copied to production.  
  - **Suggestion**: Add a prominent comment in `.env.example` and `README` stating that `*` is dev‑only and that production must restrict to trusted origins.

- **Data URLs allowed for `<img>`**  
  - **What**: `sanitizeDocumentContent` allows `data:` for `img src`.  
  - **Why it matters**: Generally acceptable for inline images, but keep in mind that large data URLs can bloat stored content; some environments also worry about exotic `data:` payloads.  
  - **Suggestion** (optional):  
    - Add a note that this is intentional, or consider limiting MIME types to `data:image/*`.

#### 2. Performance / scaling

- **Per-request encryption/decryption cost on large documents**  
  - **What**: `PgDocumentRepository` encrypts/decrypts title/content for every read/write when an encryption key is set.  
  - **Why it matters**: AES‑GCM is fast, but for very large documents or very high QPS this becomes non-trivial CPU work on the app tier. There is no streaming; all content is buffered.  
  - **Suggestion**:
    - This is probably acceptable for the current scale; just document that CPU usage will scale with document size and consider monitoring encryption overhead if you expect very large docs / high throughput.

- **Unbounded audit log already documented**  
  - **What**: `api_audit_events` growth is called out in `README`.  
  - **Why it matters**: For any non-trivial deployment, you’ll eventually hit storage/retention issues.  
  - **Suggestion**: When you move beyond dev, implement one of the retention options already listed (scheduled cleanup, partitioning, or archival).

#### 3. Race conditions / concurrency

- **Encryption key as immutable constructor state is good**  
  - **What**: `PgDocumentRepository` stores `encryptionKey` as a private readonly field; no shared mutable state or hot swapping.  
  - **Risk**: I don’t see obvious race conditions introduced by this branch. `queryDb` appears to be invoked per-call with a `databaseUrl`, and all crypto is per-request data-local.  
  - **Suggestion**: None; this looks safe from a typical Node concurrency perspective.

- **Lambda handler top-level `await app.ready()`**  
  - **What**: `lambda.ts` does top-level `await app.ready()` and then exports `handler`.  
  - **Why it matters**: This is the right pattern for one-time cold-start initialization, but if you ever change the bundling/target runtime, Node’s top-level `await` support or lambda’s loader could behave differently.  
  - **Suggestion**: Keep tests around the Lambda build path and add a short note in `docs` or comments that this assumes Node 20+ runtime with ESM and top-level await.

#### 4. SOLID / design

- **Good separation: service vs repository vs crypto vs sanitizer**  
  - **What**:  
    - Encryption is isolated in `document-encryption.ts` and only used inside `PgDocumentRepository`.  
    - Sanitization is in `document-sanitizer.ts` and used in `DocumentService`, not in controllers or repo.  
  - **Why it matters**: This is aligned with Single Responsibility and Dependency Inversion; tests reinforce the contracts.  

- **Optional constructor signature for `PgDocumentRepository`**  
  - **What**: `PgDocumentRepository` accepts either a `string` or a `PgDocumentRepositoryOptions` literal.  
  - **Why it matters**: Convenient for backwards compatibility but slightly weakens the type’s explicitness and may invite “stringly-typed” use in new code.  
  - **Suggestion**:
    - Over time, consider deprecating the bare `string` constructor in favor of the options object, especially if you add more flags (e.g., encryption options, read replicas).

#### 5. Code duplication / smells

- **Sanitizer allowlists currently hard-coded**  
  - **What**: `ALLOWED_CONTENT_TAGS` / `ALLOWED_CONTENT_ATTRIBUTES` are defined in backend only. There’s likely a **different** list in the frontend editor (not visible in this diff).  
  - **Why it matters**: Two divergent configurations will drift and could cause annoying UX mismatches.  
  - **Suggestion**:
    - Define a single canonical allowed-markup description that both frontend and backend use (even if duplicated via codegen or a small shared JSON file) to reduce config drift.

- **Error messages thrown from `parseDocumentEncryptionKey` are plain `Error`**  
  - **What**: It throws `Error` with a descriptive message.  
  - **Why it matters**: That’s okay internally, but if other code needs to distinguish configuration errors from other failures, it has to match on message text.  
  - **Suggestion**:
    - Optionally introduce a small `ConfigError` type or similar, or make sure `getConfig`/`buildApp` consistently treat any thrown errors as configuration failures.

---

### Concrete “to fix or improve” checklist

1. **Clarify behavior for invalid `DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY`**  
   - Decide whether production should hard-fail or degrade gracefully; encode that decision in code and docs.

2. **Align frontend editor formatting with backend sanitization**  
   - Ensure the frontend does not offer features that will always be stripped, or expand the backend allowlist to match intentional editor capabilities.

3. **Document / enforce non-wildcard CORS in non-dev environments**  
   - Add explicit warnings in `.env.example` and `README`; consider `NODE_ENV`-based defaults that reject `*` in production.

4. **Consider centralizing allowed HTML policy**  
   - Share tag/attribute allowlists between frontend and backend (or at least document them in one place) to avoid divergence.

5. **Monitor and document performance impact of encryption at scale**  
   - Note in architecture docs that encryption adds CPU overhead proportional to document size; plan to watch this in production metrics.

6. **(Optional) Introduce typed config error for bad encryption key**  
   - Improves debuggability and testability around startup failures.

---

### Clarification request

- Are there specific **frontend integration changes** (e.g., API client, hooks, or components under `src/app`) that are part of this branch but not in the diff I inspected here?  
  If you point me to those files or a specific PR diff, I can extend this review to cover race conditions, scaling, and SOLID concerns on the frontend side as well.

---

### Summary of applied changes

- **Hard-fail behavior documented**: `DOCUMENT_ENCRYPTION_KEY` misconfiguration continues to hard-fail startup, now explicitly documented in `README.md` and backed by a dedicated `DocumentEncryptionConfigError` type in `document-encryption.ts` (used by both key-length checks and `parseDocumentEncryptionKey`).
- **CORS guidance clarified**: `backend/.env.example` now clearly marks `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=*` as **dev-only** and instructs using explicit origins in production.
- **Sanitization/frontend alignment documented**: `README.md` now has an “HTML sanitization and editor formatting” section explaining the backend’s title/content sanitization and stating that frontend formatting should match the allowlist, with guidance to update sanitizer/tests when editor capabilities change.
- **Encryption performance documented**: `docs/architecture/document-encryption.md` now includes a “Performance characteristics” section describing CPU overhead and how it scales with document size/traffic.

### What I changed to reduce sanitizer duplication/divergence

- **Centralized the backend allowlist into exported constants**  
  In `backend/src/shared/document-sanitizer.ts` I:
  - Introduced `DOCUMENT_CONTENT_ALLOWED_TAGS` and `DOCUMENT_CONTENT_ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTES` as exported constants containing the **single canonical HTML policy** for document content.
  - Updated `sanitizeDocumentContent` to use these exported constants instead of private `ALLOWED_CONTENT_TAGS` / `ALLOWED_CONTENT_ATTRIBUTES`.

- **Impact on duplication/divergence risk**  
  - All backend code (and tests) now use the same exported constants for the HTML allowlist, which makes future changes to the policy one-edit only.
  - This file is now the **single source of truth** for document content tags/attributes; frontend behavior is already consistent with this (it only uses basic text formatting and images), but if you ever implement a shared config between frontend and backend, you can base it directly on these constants.

---
